sourceMovie.title: Decision to Leave
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Decision to Leave works as a thriller movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The thriller and mystery blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Rebecca
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Decision to Leave who respond to its thriller will find Rebecca (1940) immediately familiar. Both films share a mystery sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Twelve Monkeys
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Decision to Leave fans keep landing on Twelve Monkeys (1995) — the thriller is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Ghost
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Ghost draws from the same thriller well as Decision to Leave, channeling romance in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 1990 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Match Point
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Match Point (2005) captures the thriller appeal of Decision to Leave without simply imitating it. The romance streak running through both films is what puts Match Point at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: I See You
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller appeal of Decision to Leave — that dark edge, the mystery — translates cleanly into I See You (2019). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Body Cam
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Body Cam (2020) clearly understood what makes thriller work — the same quality that keeps Decision to Leave rewatchable. Expect the same dark rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Eternal Sunshine of the Spotless Mind (2004) is the kind of film Decision to Leave fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The romance overlap is real, but it's the bittersweet emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: Donnie Darko
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Donnie Darko shares Decision to Leave's gift for mystery — the 2001 film delivers fantasy without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

recommendations.title: Saw
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Much of what makes Decision to Leave compelling — the mystery, the horror — shows up in Saw (2004) in a slightly different form. Both films understand that dark storytelling works best when it trusts the audience.

recommendations.title: Minority Report
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The thriller energy of Decision to Leave is alive in Minority Report (2002), filtered through a different story but the same emotional register. It earns its place on this list with a tense payoff that rivals the original pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Decision to Leave?
faq.answer: Try Rebecca, Twelve Monkeys, Ghost—each shares DNA with Decision to Leave while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Decision to Leave?
faq.answer: Decision to Leave (2022) is best described as Thriller, Mystery, Romance. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Decision to Leave worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this thriller lane.
